posts.author: danaaviv (Dana Aviv - monday Sales CRM Expert)
posts.cookedHtml:
SO many amazing features…

TLDR
*Conditional coloring
*Sorting columns
*Pin columns
*Item default values
*Group & board summaries
*New Advanced filter

Hey all!
We just wanted to make sure you are all aware of the recent incredible additions we added to the platform

First, we redesigned the toolbar, to make sure you all get what you need faster and clearer.
We added a menu on its right corner for additional actions

1600×512 261 KB

Let’s go over the cool features we have there…

1. Sort:
We added the ability to sort your columns from the toolbar as well. This is extremely convenient if you want to sort multiple columns and save.

1600×562 335 KB

Of course, you can still sort from the columns themselves.
310×682 16.4 KB

2. New advanced filter:

You can filter your board using different variables!

1600×495 213 KB

Can be opened via monday.labs - Check out Ziv’s post about it from yesterday!

3. Pin columns:

You can now pin the columns you want to always stay pinned when you scroll right

1600×376 217 KB

4. Conditional coloring:

You can now apply formatting to cells that meet your defined criteria. This gives you the ability to visually emphasize or differentiate the data on your board.

1600×473 95.9 KB

Access it from the same toolbar menu:

1600×381 215 KB

and choose the color of the cells according to the conditions that you set!

1600×651 130 KB

More columns WILL be supported soon (formula, mirror, etc)
Read the article to learn more

5. Item default Value:

Ever wanted to add an item on your board and to already have its pre-made values?
Now you can set up the pre-made default values:

1600×379 213 KB

6. Summaries Summaries Summaries!

Now you can see a summary of all your columns, groups, and entire board, on the board itself! It’s like having a dashboard on the board!

Collapse your groups and you will see the summary of all your column footers on the collapsed groups:

1600×667 187 KB

We hope you enjoy these new additions, and please let us know if you have any questions! As always, you can comment here or send us an email to support@monday.com

posts.author: TheOtherPM (Paul McGregor)
posts.cookedHtml: Awesome new features. Some of these look like small features, but will have a big impact. Thanks. I also like how you are pinning these updates to the top of the community feed.

posts.author: snicho (Stephen Nicholson)
posts.cookedHtml:
Definitely some fantastic new features here. I’m not 100% convinced about the implementation of the Advanced Filter yet, but maybe it will grow on me.

The new Summaries are great. However, these really need to maintain visibility of column headings, particularly if all Groups are collapsed. Without the headings, the summary information can loose context and relevance.

Otherwise, it’s great to see the interface continuing to evolve.

posts.author: danaaviv (Dana Aviv - monday Sales CRM Expert)
posts.cookedHtml:
hey everyone! quick update regarding group and board footers. We realized they were causing some slowness on big boards with many items, so we decided to make it a “granted feature” which you can grant to yourselves via monday.labs

image1270×454 45.8 KB

We are working on fixing this issue, and it will once again be released. In the meantime, if it didn’t cause you any issues, just activate it from your account. if you see that it is causing some slowness, just deactivate it

posts.author: anon34162818 (anon34162818)
posts.cookedHtml: Pin column feature for me…pins all preceding columns, I thought / think it is only supposed to pin a single column? Anyone having the same experience? Basically if I pin a column too far to the right every preceding column is automatically pinned and you can no longer scroll right, alternatively if you pin the first column it remains pinned when scrolling right.

posts.author: caseyparnis (Casey Parnis)
posts.cookedHtml:
The custom sort functionality is excellent! To make this feature even more useful we would really appreciate either:

  1. Ideally, the board automatically applying the saved sort when a new item is added. I know that’s not currently possible even on single column sorts but it would be so helpful.
  2. If that is not possible, a way for the sort order to be saved so that you can click “Sort” somewhere without having to create the entire sort order again.

posts.author: hlopezvc (Hernan Lopez)
posts.cookedHtml:
@danaaviv Hi i want to give some feedback on conditional coloring.

The example you give sounds great when you have a board of 5 columns, but to having colored only the cell of the column that aplies to the conditions give some limits to this feature. In real case boards, to give our employees the chance to use this feature at his best, i think that the whole row must be colored in order to give more visibility and alert them of what we want to do.

Let me know what you think.

posts.author: danaaviv (Dana Aviv - monday Sales CRM Expert)
posts.cookedHtml: Great feedback, thank you! Can you please submit this in the form I have linked?
